pr.title: Match gmadventure, link, and ninja apply
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched the Adventure mode game-mode object by refactoring a repeated gm_8016A22C setup sequence in gmadventure.c into a shared static helper, then flipping melee/gm/gmadventure.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The PR also updated GALE01 local .sdata2 symbol labels from address-style gm_804DACxx names to compiler-style @NN labels. decomp.dev reported one new match: main/melee/gm/gmadventure gm_801B4974 reached 100.00%.

key_files.role: Refactored repeated Adventure setup code into gm_8016A22C_inline and used it from gm_801B4974 and inline0.
Renamed six local .sdata2 symbols at 0x804DAC18-0x804DAC2C from gm_804DACxx to @217, @218, @244, @306, @307, and @330.

what_changed: Added static helper gm_8016A22C_inline(MinorScene* arg0, UnkAdventureData* temp_r29) to gather the gm_8017E48C count, load gm_803DE650_t via gm_8017E4C4(arg0->idx), call gm_8016A22C with the same Adventure data fields, and set temp_r29->x0.xC.x11 = 1.
Replaced the inlined gm_8016A22C setup block inside gm_801B4974 with gm_8016A22C_inline(arg0, temp_r29), removing local temp_r25_2 usage there.
Replaced the same setup block inside inline0 with gm_8016A22C_inline(scene, adv_data), while preserving the preceding gm_80169520 and gm_80169540 unused calls inside the x11 guard.
Promoted melee/gm/gmadventure.c to Matching in configure.py.
Updated GALE01 .sdata2 local symbols gm_804DAC18, gm_804DAC1C, gm_804DAC20, gm_804DAC24, gm_804DAC28, and gm_804DAC2C to @217, @218, @244, @306, @307, and @330.
decomp.dev reported matched code 55.14% (+0.01%, +436 bytes), linked code 31.75% (+0.13%, +5092 bytes), linked data 29.16% (+0.16%, +1996 bytes), and gm_801B4974 improving from 99.04% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Factored a duplicated, long-argument gm_8016A22C call sequence into one helper instead of trying to separately tune two copies; this reduced local-variable pressure and made both call sites share the same byte-sensitive expression ordering.
Kept the x0.xC.x11 flag write in the helper with the setup call, matching the original logical unit and avoiding divergence between gm_801B4974 and inline0.
Preserved the apparently unused gm_80169520 and gm_80169540 calls in inline0 before the helper call, which is important for matching call order and potential side effects.
Completed the decomp workflow by updating configure.py to mark the now-matching object as Matching, which is what drove the linked-code/linkable-object metric improvement.
Let local data labels use @NN names for compiler-local .sdata2 objects instead of address-derived gm_804DACxx placeholders.
decomp_lessons: When a nearly matching function contains a repeated call setup also present in an inline helper path, extracting a shared static helper can be a matching tactic, not just a readability cleanup.

For long calls with many struct-field arguments and function-pointer calls, matching can depend on where temporaries are introduced; moving count and gm_803DE650_t lookup into a helper changed the source shape enough for gm_801B4974 to match.
Do not remove unused-looking calls during cleanup if they existed in the matching path; gm_80169520 and gm_80169540 remained in inline0 even after the actual setup was moved.
After code reaches 100%, update the build object classification from NonMatching to Matching so the object contributes to linked-code progress.
Ninja/decomp apply workflows may require symbol map cleanup: local compiler literals in .sdata2 should be represented as @NN labels when that is what the matching output expects.
naming_conventions: Compiler-local .sdata2 labels use @NN form, as shown by @217, @218, @244, @306, @307, and @330 replacing gm_804DAC18-style local placeholders.
The new helper used an address-based function name plus _inline suffix, gm_8016A22C_inline, indicating an inferred helper around the gm_8016A22C call rather than a known semantic name.
Existing unknown structure and field names were retained, including UnkAdventureData, gm_803DE650_t, xA, xD, xE, xF, x4C, x64, x68, and x0.xC.x11; the PR did not attempt semantic renaming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a small static helper to encapsulate a duplicated setup sequence when two source sites need identical generated code around a long call.

Keep argument order exactly aligned with the original call: stage data from gm_803DE650_t, character kind/color/cpu_level from UnkAdventureData, function-pointer results x4C/x64/x68, and gm_8017BE8C(temp_r25_2->xA).
Move local temporaries such as count and temp_r25_2 into the helper to affect lifetime/register allocation at the caller.
Preserve guarded side-effect calls before refactored setup code rather than treating them as dead just because their assigned UNK_T variables are unused.
Update symbols.txt local labels after applying matching output so local floats/bytes in .sdata2 line up with generated assembly labels.
follow_up_queries: Search gmadventure.c for other duplicated gm_8016A22C setup blocks that might benefit from the same helper pattern.
Audit remaining local .sdata2 gm_804DACxx-style symbols and decide whether they should be compiler-local @NN labels.
Investigate whether gm_8016A22C_inline can receive a semantic name once UnkAdventureData and gm_803DE650_t fields are better understood.
Review remaining NonMatching gm files near gmadventure in configure.py, especially gmclassic and gmallstar, for similar helper-extraction opportunities.
